title: The reactions of chlorine
summary: 9701 AS chlorine chemistry — disproportionation in water and alkali, water treatment, and free-radical substitution.
learningObjectives: Write equations for chlorine dissolving in water.
Describe reactions with cold and hot sodium hydroxide.
Explain use of chlorine in water treatment.
Link chlorine to free-radical halogenation of alkanes.

simpleExplanation.title: Cl₂ in water and alkali
simpleExplanation.summary: Chlorine disproportionates in water to HCl and HOCl (bleach). With NaOH, products depend on temperature and concentration.
steps: Cl₂ + H₂O ⇌ HCl + HOCl — disinfectant HOCl kills bacteria.
Cold dilute NaOH: Cl₂ + 2OH⁻ → Cl⁻ + ClO⁻ + H₂O.
Hot conc. NaOH: forms Cl⁻ and ClO₃⁻.

sections.content: **Chlorine reactions** (11.4) combine **redox** (6.1) and **environmental chemistry**. Show **oxidation state changes** in disproportionation.
